seoTitle: South Kumminin vs South Lake — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Kumminin and South Lake. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Kumminin vs South Lake: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Kumminin and South Lake.
heading: South Kumminin vs South Lake — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Kumminin, Western Australia and South Lake, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Kumminin has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to South Lake (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Kumminin or South Lake safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Kumminin has a flood risk score of 58/100, while South Lake scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Kumminin scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. South Lake scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Kumminin or South Lake?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Kumminin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
